perturbation.original_text: For a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Discloser's Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose publish, or disseminate, but in no event shall Recipient use less than reasonable care to protect Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Discloser's Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose publish, or disseminate, but in no event shall Recipient use less than reasonable care to protect Discloser's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the duration of confidentiality from three years to one year creates an in-text contradiction within section 2. This contradicts the general obligation for confidentiality which assumes a three-year period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date above. Either party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other. Upon termination of this Agreement, or earlier upon Discloser's request, Recipient shall promptly return or destroy all documents and tangible items in its possession which contain any part of the Confidential Information of Discloser. In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a confidentiality period of three years, while the changed text reduces this to one year. The contradicted text states that 'all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms' upon termination. The change from three years to one year directly contradicts the continuous obligation based on the original terms, which implicitly assumed a three-year confidentiality period. This creates a clear conflict as the agreement now has conflicting confidentiality durations.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may use the Confidential Information solely for evaluation purposes in connection with Recipient business discussions with Discloser. If software is provided Recipient under this Agreement, Recipient agrees not to reverse compile or disassemble the software to discover the human perceivable portions of the code.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose. If software is provided Recipient under this Agreement, Recipient is permitted to reverse compile or disassemble the software to discover the human perceivable portions of the code.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the allowed use of Confidential Information from 'solely for evaluation purposes' to 'for any purpose', and allowing reverse compiling of the software directly contradicts the original restrictions. The original clause was restrictive in nature, and the new version is permissive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient certifies that the Confidential Information will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein and will not be rented, leased, sold, sublicensed, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Recipient shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Recipient to use the Confidential Information for 'any purpose' and reverse compile/disassemble software, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text' which states the Confidential Information will 'only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein' and limits its use. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document as the changed text broadens the scope of permissible actions beyond the original restrictions, and also allows reverse compiling of the software.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the ownership clause to state that Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year directly contradicts the original statement that 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.' This alteration creates significant uncertainty about ownership rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership: All Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year. The contradicted text states the exact same thing: 'Ownership: All Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year.' Based on the explanation provided, which says 'Modifying the ownership clause to state that Confidential Information becomes the property of the Recipient after one year directly contradicts the original statement that 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.' This alteration creates significant uncertainty about ownership rights' a clear contradiction is introduced between the original text, which assigns ownership to the Discloser, and the changed text, which assigns it to the Recipient after one year.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes the Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser's Confidential Information. However, the foregoing does not give Recipient the right to disclose (except as set forth in Section 3) the financial, statistical, or personnel information or the business plans of Discloser, and the foregoing shall not be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyright or patents.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes the Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser's Confidential Information. However, the foregoing gives Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel information or the business plans of Discloser, and the foregoing shall be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyright or patents.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the restriction on disclosing financial, statistical, and personnel information, and granting a license under copyright or patents, directly contradicts the original intent of the clause, which explicitly prohibited these actions. The clause now allows actions that were previously forbidden, creating an obvious contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Freedom of Use. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary, Recipient shall be free to use for any purposes the Residuals resulting from access to or work with Discloser's Confidential Information. However, the foregoing gives Recipient the right to disclose the financial, statistical, or personnel information or the business plans of Discloser, and the foregoing shall be deemed to grant to either party a license under the other party's copyright or patents. The term "Residuals" means information which may be retained in non-tangible form by person who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how or techniques contained therein. Neither party shall have any obligation to limit or restrict the assignment or reassignment of personnel. Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement, the parties acknowledge that the communications hereunder will not serve to impair the right of either party to independently develop, make, use, procure or market products or services now or in the future that may be similar to or competitive with those offered by Discloser, nor require Recipient to disclose any planning or other information to Discloser. This Section shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent of the clause. The original text states that the Recipient *does not* have the right to disclose financial, statistical, or personnel information. The changed text states the Recipient *does* have that right. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, as both were taken from the same location after the change was made, highlighting the contradiction. This constitutes a clear and direct conflict within the document, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall survive and continue to bind Recipient in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of termination of this Agreement, all obligations of confidentiality shall cease immediately.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the clause to state that confidentiality obligations cease upon termination directly contradicts the original provision that these obligations survive termination. This creates a fundamental conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligation of Confidentiality. For a period of one (1) year from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Discloser's Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose publish, or disseminate, but in no event shall Recipient use less than reasonable care to protect Discloser's Confidential Information. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees and information which may be retained in non-tangible form by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that confidentiality obligations survive termination. The changed text states that confidentiality obligations cease immediately upon termination. The contradicted text specifies a one-year period for confidentiality obligations. The changed text directly contradicts both the original text and the contradicted text, as it eliminates any post-termination confidentiality obligations, creating a clear conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Neither party may assign its rights or delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement without prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may assign its rights or delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement at any time, without consent.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the assignment clause to allow assignment without consent directly contradicts the original clause that requires prior written consent for any assignment or delegation. This creates a conflict regarding the transferability of rights and obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees. The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisdiction and venue in any such proceeding. b. Export Constraints. Recipient certifies that the Confidential Information will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein and will not be rented, leased, sold, sublicensed, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Recipient shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government. c. Either party may assign its rights or delegate its duties or obligations under this Agreement at any time, without consent. The parties acknowledge that they have read this Agreement, understand it, and agree to be bound by the terms and conditions. Further, they agree that the complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties relating to this subject shall consist of this Agreement. Any reproduction of this Agreement by reliable means will be considered an original of this document. This Agreement is executed in English.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither party could assign its rights without prior written consent, but the changed text states that either party may assign its rights at any time, without consent. This directly contradicts the original clause. The contradicted text then repeats the changed text regarding assignment of rights without consent, therefore the modification creates a direct conflict, leading to an in-text contradiction. The change allows for assignment without consent, conflicting with a previous requirement for consent. This fits the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction, since the clause appears in multiple locations (original text and contradicted text) with conflicting implications (with consent vs without consent).
